factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable, with a slight decrease from the prior year.

evidence.text: Free cash flow for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, decreased $4 million from the same period in 2020, primarily due to changes in trade working capital and higher capital expenditures.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow decreased slightly, but the change is relatively small and stable.

factors.summary: The supply chain is disrupted due to COVID-19, leading to delays and limited deliveries.

evidence.text: COVID-19 has also caused disruption in our supply chain, caused delays in, and limited the ability of, the U.S. Government and other customers to perform, including causing delays in contract award decisions, and caused other unpredictable events.
evidence.subsection: We face risks related to health epidemics, pandemics, and similar outbreaks, and our business has been and will continue to be adversely affected by the COVID-19 pandemic .

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: COVID-19 disruption caused significant delays and supply chain issues.

factors.summary: International defense sales pipeline is expanding with unmanned systems products and services.

evidence.text: Unmanned systems provides design, autonomy, manufacturing, testing, operations, and sustainment of unmanned systems, including unmanned underwater vehicles and unmanned surface vessels. Serving customers in more than 30 countries.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expanding international sales pipeline indicates growth opportunity.

factors.summary: Talent retention is a concern due to potential employee attrition from vaccine mandates.

evidence.text: Our implementation of these requirements will result in employee attrition to some extent, including attrition of critical skilled labor, and difficulty meeting future labor requirements. If attrition is significant, our operations and ability to execute our contracts could be materially impacted.
evidence.subsection: We face risks related to health epidemics, pandemics, and similar outbreaks, and our business has been and will continue to be adversely affected by the COVID-19 pandemic .

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Potential employee attrition from vaccine mandates poses a risk.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include delays and cost overruns on major programs such as the Block IV boats of the Virginia class submarine program.

evidence.text: Unfavorable cumulative catch-up margin adjustments on the Block IV boats of the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program also included $16 million from delay and disruption directly attributable to COVID-19 Events due to lower employee attendance, a shortage of critical skills, and out-of-sequence work.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Delays and cost overruns on major programs are significant risks.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable with no evidence of successful price increases or discounts.

evidence.text: Our contracts typically fall into one of four categories: firm fixed-price, fixed-price incentive, cost-type, and time and materials.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No evidence of price changes suggests stable pricing power.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are ongoing, but savings are offset by increased costs related to COVID-19.

evidence.text: We have aggressively managed our response to the uncertainties regarding COVID-19 Events, and we have incurred costs to respond to COVID-19 Events, including paid leave, quarantining employees, vaccinations, and recurring facility cleaning.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction efforts are ongoing, but offset by COVID-19 related costs.

factors.summary: Productivity is challenged due to workforce attendance issues and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: We continue to experience decreases in workforce attendance and challenges meeting our hiring requirements, which has impacted our operations due to delay and disruption from a shortage of critical skills and out-of-sequence work.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Workforce attendance issues and supply chain problems hinder productivity.

factors.summary: Market share is stable with no evidence of gain or loss.

evidence.text: We are America’s largest military shipbuilding company and a provider of professional services to partners in government and industry.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Market share is stable with no evidence of gain or loss.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are stable with no significant changes in the industry landscape.

evidence.text: We are America’s largest military shipbuilding company and a provider of professional services to partners in government and industry.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Competitive dynamics are stable with no significant changes in the industry landscape.

factors.summary: Differentiation is maintained through technological and operational expertise in shipbuilding and professional services.

evidence.text: Our Technical Solutions segment provides a wide range of professional services and products, including defense and federal solutions (DFS), nuclear and environmental services, and unmanned systems.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Differentiation is maintained through technological and operational expertise in shipbuilding and professional services.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is high with significant reliance on U.S. Government contracts, posing dependency risks.

evidence.text: We conduct most of our business with the U.S. Government, primarily the Department of Defense (DoD).

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: High customer concentration with significant reliance on U.S. Government contracts poses dependency risks.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as vaccine mandates, are impacting operations and labor costs.

evidence.text: On September 9, 2021, President Biden issued an executive order requiring all employers with U.S. Government contracts to ensure that their U.S.-based employees, contractors, and subcontractors that work on or in support of U.S. Government contracts are fully vaccinated by December 8, 2021.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Regulatory changes, such as vaccine mandates, are impacting operations and labor costs.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure exists regarding business interruption insurance claims related to COVID-19.

evidence.text: We filed a complaint in state court in Vermont seeking a judgment declaring that our business interruption and other losses associated with COVID-19 are covered by our property insurance program. We also initiated arbitration proceedings against other reinsurers seeking similar relief.
evidence.subsection: Critical Accounting Policies, Estimates, and Judgments

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Litigation exposure exists regarding business interruption insurance claims related to COVID-19.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed, showing a positive increase in certain segments such as submarines and aircraft carriers, along with slight increases in product sales and service revenues, but also indicating a decline in service revenues in some periods.

evidence.text: Newport News revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $303 million, or 8%, from the same period in 2020, primarily driven by higher revenues in submarines and aircraft carriers.
Sales and service revenues increased $24 million, or 1%, for the three months ended September 30, 2021, compared with the same period in 2020.

Product sales for the three months ended September 30, 2021, increased $2 million from the same period in 2020. Service revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2021, increased $22 million, or 4%, compared with the same period in 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Mixed organic growth with positive increases in certain segments despite some declines.

factors.summary: The backlog_orders factor shows mixed trends across different subsections of the SEC filing. While the BACKLOG section indicates a positive growth in total backlog from $46.0 billion to $50.1 billion, the Sales and Service Revenues section also points to growth, particularly in surface combatants and amphibious assault ships. However, the Business Environment section suggests stability in the backlog, with no clear indication of growth or decline.

evidence.text: Total backlog as of September 30, 2021, and December 31, 2020, was approximately $50.1 billion and $46.0 billion, respectively.

Ingalls revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $21 million, or 1%, from the same period in 2020, primarily driven by higher revenues in surface combatants and amphibious assault ships.
We conduct most of our business with the U.S. Government, primarily the Department of Defense (DoD).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Backlog shows positive growth in total backlog and specific ship types.

factors.summary: The contract backlog is growing, driven by new orders and contributions from various shipbuilding programs. However, there is a conflicting indication of stability in the business environment.

evidence.text: The value of new contract awards during the nine months ended September 30, 2021, was approximately $7.1 billion, comprised primarily of awards for the RCOH of the USS John C. Stennis (CVN 74), construction of a 10th boat of the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program, and construction of John F. Lehma n (DDG 137).

We have contracts to construct the following Arleigh Burke class (DDG 51) destroyers: Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG 121), Lenah H. Sutcliffe Higbee (DDG 123), Jack H. Lucas (DDG 125), Ted Stevens (DDG 128), Jeremiah Denton (DDG 129), George M. Neal (DDG 131), Sam Nunn (DDG 133), Thad Cochran (DDG 135), and John F. Lehman (DDG 137).

Ingalls revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $21 million, or 1%, from the same period in 2020, primarily driven by higher revenues in surface combatants and amphibious assault ships, partially offset by lower revenues in the Legend class NSC program.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The contract backlog is growing due to new orders and shipbuilding programs, indicating increased business activity.

factors.summary: The defense budget is currently experiencing mixed trends, with growth in certain areas but also facing potential long-term reductions. While there are signs of increased spending on core programs, such as the Navy's submarine initiative, there are concerns about future funding uncertainties and the impact of potential budget cuts on the industry.

evidence.text: The U.S. Navy has initiated the design process for the new class of submarines, and, in early 2017, the DoD signed the acquisition decision memorandum approving the Columbia class program’s Milestone B, which formally authorizes the program’s entry into the engineering and manufacturing development phase.

Long-term funding for certain programs in which we participate may be reduced, delayed, or canceled. In addition, spending cuts and/or reprioritization of defense investment could adversely affect the viability of our suppliers, subcontractors, and employee base.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The defense budget shows mixed trends with growth in some areas but concerns about future reductions and funding uncertainties.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed margin performance. While some segments show expansion in operating margins, others face compression due to higher costs, supply chain issues, and unfavorable adjustments. The overall impact of these factors is creating pressure on profitability.

evidence.text: Gross and operating margins are expanding, particularly in the Newport News segment.
Segment operating income (loss) 88 79 9 11% 257 105 152 145% As a percentage of segment sales 6.5 % 5.8 % 6.2 % 2.7 %
evidence.subsection: We face risks related to health epidemics, pandemics, and similar outbreaks, and our business has been and will continue to be adversely affected by the COVID-19 pandemic .

evidence.text: These impacts may continue, and the cost increases may not be fully recoverable under our contracts or adequately covered by insurance, which could impact our profitability.
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Service Revenues

evidence.text: Cost of product sales as a percentage of product sales increased from 81.7% for the three months ended September 30, 2020, to 85.4% for the three months ended September 30, 2021. The increase was primarily due to an unfavorable change in the Operating FAS/CAS Adjustment, lower performance in unmanned systems, and lower risk retirement on Bougainville (LHA 8), partially offset by higher risk retirements on the RCOH of USS George Washington (CVN 73), Block IV boats of the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program, and USS Frank E. Peterson Jr. (DDG 121).

Operating income 118 222 (104) (47) % Other income (expense) Interest expense (24) (27) 3 11 % Non-operating retirement benefit 45 29 16 55 % Other, net 2 2 — — % Federal and foreign income taxes (6) 4 (10) (250) % Net earnings $ 147 $ 222 $ (75) (34) %

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company experiences mixed margin performance with some expansion and compression, leading to overall pressure on profitability.

factors.summary: Program execution is facing significant challenges, including delays and cost overruns, primarily due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. The Virginia class submarine program, particularly its Block IV boats, has been notably affected, with delays and disruptions linked to lower employee attendance, skill shortages, and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: COVID-19 has already impacted our business and results of operations, and the ultimate impact of COVID-19 on our operations and financial performance in future periods, including our ability to execute our programs on the expected schedule, remains uncertain and will depend on future COVID-19 related developments.
evidence.subsection: We face risks related to health epidemics, pandemics, and similar outbreaks, and our business has been and will continue to be adversely affected by the COVID-19 pandemic .

evidence.text: Unfavorable cumulative catch-up margin adjustments on the Block IV boats of the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program also included $16 million from delay and disruption directly attributable to COVID-19 Events due to lower employee attendance, a shortage of critical skills, and out-of-sequence work.

We continue to experience decreases in workforce attendance and challenges meeting our hiring requirements, which has impacted our operations due to delay and disruption from a shortage of critical skills and out-of-sequence work.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Program execution faces significant challenges due to delays, cost overruns, and impacts from the COVID-19 pandemic.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher absentee rates, vaccine mandates, and the attrition of critical skilled labor, leading to margin pressure and challenges in meeting future labor requirements.

evidence.text: Higher absentee rates attributable to COVID-19, including because of illness, quarantines, government actions, facility closures, or other restrictions resulting from COVID-19, have impacted and may continue to impact performance on our contracts and have increased and may continue to increase our costs.
evidence.subsection: We face risks related to health epidemics, pandemics, and similar outbreaks, and our business has been and will continue to be adversely affected by the COVID-19 pandemic .

evidence.text: Our implementation of these requirements will result in employee attrition to some extent, including attrition of critical skilled labor, and difficulty meeting future labor requirements.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to higher absentee rates, vaccine mandates, and attrition of skilled labor, causing margin pressure.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with slight revenue growth in certain segments but no significant overall acceleration or deceleration.

evidence.text: Sales and service revenues increased $24 million, or 1%, for the three months ended September 30, 2021, compared with the same period in 2020.
We conduct most of our business with the U.S. Government, primarily the Department of Defense (DoD).

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends show slight revenue growth in some segments but no significant overall acceleration or deceleration.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products and segments, which is benefiting overall performance. This shift is evident in both the Segment Operating Income and Sales and Service Revenues subsections.

evidence.text: Segment operating income for the three months ended September 30, 2021, was $163 million, compared with segment operating income of $162 million for the same period in 2020. The increase was primarily due to higher risk retirement on the RCOH of USS George Washington (CVN 73), and Block IV boats of the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program.

Technical Solutions product sales increased $5 million for the three months ended September 30, 2021, primarily as a result of higher volumes in DFS due to the acquisition of Alion, partially offset by lower volumes in unmanned systems.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products, benefiting overall performance and profitability.

factors.summary: The end-market momentum for the company's products is mixed. While surface combatants and amphibious assault ships are experiencing growth, the Legend class NSC program is declining. Additionally, the broader defense industry faces challenges due to budget uncertainties and the ongoing impacts of COVID-19, which further complicates the business environment.

evidence.text: Ingalls revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $21 million, or 1%, from the same period in 2020, primarily driven by higher revenues in surface combatants and amphibious assault ships, partially offset by lower revenues in the Legend class NSC program.

The federal budget environment remains a significant long-term risk. Considerable uncertainty exists regarding how future budget and program decisions will develop and what challenges budget changes will present for the defense industry.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: End-market momentum is mixed with growth in some areas but decline in others, compounded by budget uncertainties and COVID-19 impacts.

factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed performance across different shipbuilding programs. While surface combatants and amphibious assault ships are experiencing growth, other programs like the Legend class NSC and surface combatant services are seeing declines. Submarines and aircraft carriers are also reporting growth in certain segments.

evidence.text: Ingalls revenues for the nine months ended September 30, 2021, increased $21 million, or 1%, from the same period in 2020, primarily driven by higher revenues in surface combatants and amphibious assault ships, partially offset by lower revenues in the Legend class NSC program.

Newport News product sales increased $34 million and $374 million for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2021, respectively, primarily as a result of higher volumes in submarines and aircraft carriers. Ingalls service revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2021, decreased $14 million, primarily as a result of lower volumes in surface combatant services.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly in raw materials and commodities, as well as labor, due to factors such as vaccine mandates and supply chain disruptions. The company is partially offsetting these cost increases through higher risk retirements on certain programs.

evidence.text: Cost of product sales as a percentage of product sales increased from 81.7% for the three months ended September 30, 2020, to 85.4% for the three months ended September 30, 2021. The increase was primarily due to an unfavorable change in the Operating FAS/CAS Adjustment, lower performance in unmanned systems, and lower risk retirement on Bougainville (LHA 8), partially offset by higher risk retirements on the RCOH of USS George Washington (CVN 73), Block IV boats of the Virginia class (SSN 774) submarine program, and USS Frank E. Peterson Jr. (DDG 121).
evidence.subsection: Cost of Sales and Service Revenues

evidence.text: It is currently not possible to predict with any certainty the impact the executive order will have on our workforce. As a U.S. Government contractor, we are currently requiring all U.S.-based employees, contractors, and subcontractors that service or support our U.S. Government contracts to be fully vaccinated in accordance with the guidelines of the Safer Federal Workforce Task Force.
